title: CSV Summary Report CLI
summary: A Python command-line interface tool that reads a CSV file and generates a summary report with statistical information such as row count, column count, data types, and basic statistics for numerical columns.

user_stories.title: User can run the CLI and provide a CSV file path
user_stories.description: The user should be able to execute the CLI tool and pass the path to a CSV file as an argument.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI accepts a file path as a command-line argument
The tool validates that the provided path is a valid CSV file
If the file is invalid, the tool displays an error message

user_stories.title: User can view a summary of the CSV data
user_stories.description: The tool should generate a summary report containing key statistics about the CSV data.
acceptance_criteria: The summary includes the number of rows and columns
The summary lists the data types of each column
For numerical columns, the summary includes min, max, average, and standard deviation

user_stories.title: User can choose output format
user_stories.description: The user should have the option to output the summary in either text or JSON format.
acceptance_criteria: The CLI supports a flag to specify the output format (e.g., --format=json)
If no format is specified, the default is text
The tool correctly formats the output based on the selected format

user_stories.title: User can handle errors gracefully
user_stories.description: The tool should handle common errors such as missing files, invalid CSV formats, and unsupported data types.
acceptance_criteria: The tool provides clear and helpful error messages
The tool exits with a non-zero exit code on failure
The tool does not crash on invalid input

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The tool should process CSV files up to 10MB efficiently without excessive memory usage
The CLI should have a simple and intuitive command-line interface with help documentation
The tool should handle malformed CSV files by skipping invalid lines and continuing processing
The code should be well-documented and follow PEP8 style guidelines

constraints: The tool must be implemented in Python and should not require external dependencies beyond the standard library.
assumptions: The CSV files will be properly formatted and contain headers. The tool will not support complex data types like dates or nested structures.
out_of_scope: Support for binary data, encryption, or cloud storage integration. Advanced statistical analysis beyond basic metrics.
